parameters.content:
## Publish new Google Drive videos to social media with an AI caption via PostStack

### How it works
Drop a finished video into a Drive folder and walk away: the trigger fires on the new file, the workflow shares it with a public link (PostStack ingests the file once, immediately), OpenAI writes a caption + hashtags from the file name in your brand voice, and the video is published through [PostStack](https://poststack.techskills.academy) - a self-hosted social media publishing platform - to every channel you list, one API.

### Setup
1. In PostStack create an API key (**Settings → API keys**). API access is a PostStack Pro feature.
2. Copy the channel ids you want to post to from **Channels** (video-capable channels: Facebook, Instagram Reels).
3. Fill in **Configuration (EDIT ME)**: PostStack URL, API key, comma-separated channel ids and your brand voice.
4. Connect your Google account on **Watch the Folder** and **Share the Video**, pick the folder to watch, and connect OpenAI on **Write the Caption**.
5. Activate - and name your video files descriptively (the caption is written from the file name).

### Customization tips
Want richer captions? Chain a transcription step (OpenAI Whisper) between **Share the Video** and **Write the Caption** and feed the transcript into the prompt. The sharing step grants anyone-with-link read access - swap it for your own hosting if that's not acceptable.

## Catch & Share
Fires on a new file in the folder and makes it fetchable via a public link.

## Write with AI
OpenAI writes the caption + hashtags from the file name, in your brand voice.

## Publish via PostStack
Resolves your channels, creates one video post per channel and publishes each.

nodes.name: Watch the Folder
nodes.notes: Point at the Drive folder where finished videos land. Connect your Google credentials here.

nodes.name: Configuration (EDIT ME)

assignments.name: brandVoice
assignments.value: You write for a practical, no-hype tech brand. Short sentences. One emoji max.

nodes.notes: channelIds: comma-separated ids from PostStack -> Channels (video-capable).

nodes.name: Share the Video
nodes.notes: Grants anyone-with-link read access so PostStack can ingest the file. Revoke later if you like - PostStack stores its own copy at publish time.

nodes.name: Write the Caption

values.content: ={{ 'You are a social media copywriter. ' + $('Configuration (EDIT ME)').item.json.brandVoice + ' Reply with STRICT JSON only, no markdown fences: {"caption": "post text, max 400 chars, no hashtags inside", "hashtags": "#three #relevant #tags"}' }}
=Write a social media caption for a video file named: {{ $('Watch the Folder').item.json.name }}

nodes.notes: Connect your OpenAI credentials. For richer captions, chain a Whisper transcription step and feed the transcript in.

nodes.name: Parse the Draft
nodes.notes: The uc?export=download link serves the raw file for public files - what PostStack's media ingest needs.

nodes.name: Create Post in PostStack
nodes.notes: videoUrl instead of mediaUrl - PostStack ingests and stores the video at create/publish time.

nodes.name: Prepare Publish
nodes.notes: Pairs each created post with its target channel. URLs built here - n8n template validation dislikes multi-part expressions in URL fields.

nodes.name: Publish Now
nodes.notes: Change when to an ISO timestamp to schedule instead of publishing immediately. Pair with the post-alerts workflow to get notified about the outcome.
